Citizens may enter visa-free for up to 30 days per 60-day period (different from the standard 90-day rule), based on the 2013 bilateral interstate agreement. EU has pressured BiH to cancel this agreement as part of EU accession harmonization, but as of June 2026 the regime remains in force. Multiple analysts note BiH has declined to cancel it despite EU pressure (unlike Montenegro, which cancelled its own Russian visa-free regime in 2026).

visa_policy.notes: Bosnia and Herzegovina's visa policy is governed by the Law on Movement and Stay of Aliens and Asylum, and the Decision on Visas. Citizens of EU/Schengen member states may enter using national ID cards instead of passports. Bosnia and Herzegovina does not operate an e-visa or ETA system. No official nationality-enumerated list was accessible directly from mvp.gov.ba due to certificate errors; data compiled from official BiH embassy websites (Norway, USA, Belgium) which are government-operated embassy domains. Diplomatic/official passport exemptions apply separately for Algeria, Armenia, China, Cuba, Egypt, Indonesia, Iran, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Morocco, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Tunisia, and Ukraine - these are classified under conditional_access as passport_type entries. Kuwait had visa-free access but Bosnia and Herzegovina reintroduced the visa requirement for Kuwaiti nationals effective October 1, 2025, as part of EU alignment obligations. Bahrain, Oman, and Saudi Arabia have a SEASONAL visa-free waiver only (June 1 – September 30 annually, up to 30 days per trip) - this is NOT a year-round 90-day regime and is classified under conditional_access. UAE has a separate permanent bilateral MoU (November 2023) granting year-round 90-day visa-free access. UK-issued visas and residence permits do NOT qualify for the credential-based waiver. Bosnia and Herzegovina committed to terminating one visa-free agreement per year in 2025–2027 as part of EU accession harmonization.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid multiple-entry visa issued by a Schengen Agreement signatory state. The visa must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid residence permit issued by a Schengen Agreement signatory state. The permit must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid multiple-entry visa issued by an EU member state. The visa must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid residence permit issued by an EU member state. The permit must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid multiple-entry US visa. The visa must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. UK-issued visas explicitly DO NOT qualify (post-Brexit). One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Nationals of countries with whom Bosnia and Herzegovina has a visa regime may enter without a BiH visa for up to 30 days if they hold a valid US permanent residence permit. The permit must be valid for at least 30 days beyond the date of entry. One continuous stay cannot exceed 30 days.

Citizens of Bahrain may enter Bosnia and Herzegovina visa-free ONLY during the tourist season: June 1 to September 30 each calendar year, for a maximum of 30 days per trip. This is an annual seasonal waiver adopted by the Council of Ministers; it is NOT a year-round arrangement. Outside this window, a visa is required. The 2026 season (June 1 - September 30 2026) was confirmed renewed by the BiH Ministry of Foreign Affairs via official announcement (April 2026).

Citizens of Oman may enter Bosnia and Herzegovina visa-free ONLY during the tourist season: June 1 to September 30 each calendar year, for a maximum of 30 days per trip. This is an annual seasonal waiver adopted by the Council of Ministers; it is NOT a year-round arrangement. Outside this window, a visa is required. The 2026 season (June 1 - September 30 2026) was confirmed renewed by the BiH Ministry of Foreign Affairs via official announcement (April 2026).

Citizens of Saudi Arabia may enter Bosnia and Herzegovina visa-free ONLY during the tourist season: June 1 to September 30 each calendar year, for a maximum of 30 days per trip. This is an annual seasonal waiver adopted by the Council of Ministers; it is NOT a year-round arrangement. Outside this window, a visa is required. Note: Saudi Arabia also has diplomatic/service passport exemptions separately. The 2026 season (June 1 - September 30 2026) was confirmed renewed by the BiH Ministry of Foreign Affairs via official announcement (April 2026).
Holders of Algerian diplomatic passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Armenian diplomatic passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of Chinese diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary/business-civil passport holders are separately visa-free per bilateral agreement.
Holders of Cuban diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Egyptian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Indonesian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Iranian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Jordanian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Kazakhstani diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Moroccan diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.
Holders of Pakistani diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of Russian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders are separately visa-free for up to 30 days per 60-day period.

Holders of Saudi Arabian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders have only a seasonal visa waiver (June 1 - September 30, 30 days).
Holders of Tunisian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders require a visa.

Holders of Ukrainian diplomatic and official/service passports are exempt from visa requirements for Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ordinary passport holders are separately visa-free for up to 30 days per 60-day period.

cbi.notes: Bosnia and Herzegovina does not operate a formal citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. The Law on Citizenship of Bosnia and Herzegovina (1997) contains an Article 15 provision for 'exceptional naturalization' of persons considered 'of particular benefit to Bosnia and Herzegovina,' which can include investors and business owners making significant economic contributions or creating substantial employment. However, this is a discretionary provision requiring Council of Ministers approval, not a structured CBI program with defined investment thresholds. No official government website advertises or administers a CBI program.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Based on Real Estate Ownership
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who own real estate in Bosnia and Herzegovina may apply for a temporary residence permit. No minimum property value is specified in law. The applicant must also demonstrate a 'real and lasting connection' to Bosnia and Herzegovina (e.g., origin, children's education, pension receipt, or investment in economic activities). Issued for up to 1 year, renewable. After 5 consecutive years of temporary residence, permanent residence may be applied for. Administrative fee: 150 BAM for issuance, 200 BAM for permanent residence.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Based on Company/Business Establishment (Work Without Work Permit)
programs.notes: Founders of a company or enterprise registered in Bosnia and Herzegovina may apply for temporary residence on the basis of work without a work permit (business founder category). Minimum registered capital in FBiH entity is 1,000 BAM (approx. EUR 511) for a d.o.o. company. No higher investment threshold is set for this residence category. Temporary residence valid up to 1 year, renewable. Path to permanent residence after 5 uninterrupted years of temporary residence.

agreements: CEFTA (Central European Free Trade Agreement) - trade liberalization, not freedom of movement

validation_notes: REMOVED Kuwait (KWT) from visa_free: BiH Council of Ministers reintroduced visa requirement for Kuwaiti nationals effective October 1, 2025, as part of EU accession harmonization. Kuwait was incorrectly listed as visa_free (90 days) in the prior record.

RECLASSIFIED Bahrain (BHR) from visa_free to conditional_access (basis=seasonal): The prior record incorrectly showed Bahrain as having a year-round 90-day visa-free regime. In fact, it is a SEASONAL waiver only (June 1 - September 30, max 30 days per trip), annually renewed by Council of Ministers decision. The prior notes even stated '30-day seasonal' but the classification was wrong.
RECLASSIFIED Oman (OMN) from visa_free to conditional_access (basis=seasonal): Same reason as Bahrain - the regime is seasonal only (June 1 - September 30, 30 days), not year-round 90 days.

RECLASSIFIED Saudi Arabia (SAU) from visa_free to conditional_access (basis=seasonal): Same reason - seasonal waiver only (June 1 - September 30, 30 days). Saudi Arabia retains diplomatic/service passport exemption in conditional_access.

UPDATED UAE (ARE) source_url: Changed from Norway embassy source to official UAE MoFA announcement confirming the November 6, 2023 bilateral MoU; updated notes to clarify this is a permanent year-round arrangement.
REMOVED the redundant consolidated conditional_access entry that duplicated the individual credential entries with issuer='Schengen|EU|US' - these are now expressed as separate individual entries.
UPDATED Russia notes: Added note about EU pressure to cancel the regime; confirmed the ordinary passport visa-free (30 days/60-day period) remains in force as of June 2026.
CORRECTED visa_on_arrival notes: Added '10 designated land border crossings' to the list of locations where exceptional border visas can be issued.

2026-06-27 RE-VALIDATION: Re-fetched Norway embassy (bhembassy.no) - page still lists Kuwait in the general visa-free list, but that page is outdated and has not been updated to reflect the October 1, 2025 Council of Ministers decision reinstating visa requirements for Kuwait. Kuwait removal from visa_free is confirmed correct by multiple authoritative secondary sources citing that Council of Ministers decision. No other changes found in the visa_free list that require correction.

2026-06-27 UPDATED Singapore entry notes: Both the Norway embassy live fetch and Wikipedia confirm Singapore in the general ordinary-passport visa-free category. Removed the discrepancy flag - the Brussels embassy page appears to be the outlier; Norway embassy and Wikipedia are consistent.

2026-06-27 CONFIRMED Russia regime still in force: Verified via multiple June 2026 sources that BiH has not cancelled the visa-free arrangement with Russia despite EU pressure. Montenegro is ending its Russian visa-free regime in 2026 but BiH has not taken the same step.
2026-06-27 FIXED typo in sources_checked: 'uae-boston' corrected to 'uae-bosnia' in the mofa.gov.ae URL.

2026-06-29 RE-VALIDATION: Re-fetched Norway embassy (bhembassy.no) — page still lists Kuwait in the general visa-free list AND lists Bahrain in the 90-day general list; this page is confirmed outdated and has not been updated to reflect (a) the October 1 2025 Kuwait visa reintroduction or (b) the Bahrain/Oman/Saudi Arabia seasonal-only classification. The Norway embassy page cannot be relied upon for recent policy changes. Re-checked Brussels embassy (bhembassy.be) — similarly outdated (references 2012 rulebooks), does not include UAE, omits recent changes. Current record's Kuwait removal and Bahrain/Oman/Saudi Arabia seasonal reclassification are confirmed correct.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED Bahrain/Oman/Saudi Arabia 2026 seasonal waiver renewed: The BiH MFA official Twitter/X account (@mfa_bih, post ID 1914670295970963821, April 2026) confirmed the Council of Ministers adopted a decision to renew the seasonal visa-free waiver for Bahrain, Oman, and Saudi Arabia for the summer 2026 season (June 1 - September 30 2026, 30 days per trip). The source_url for these three conditional_access entries continues to point to the 2025 mvp.gov.ba announcement as the baseline; the 2026 renewal is confirmed via MFA Twitter and Wego (updated April 20, 2026). Added x.com/mfa_bih source to source_urls.

2026-06-29 CONFIRMED Russia visa-free regime still in force: As of June 2026, Bosnia and Herzegovina has not cancelled the visa-free arrangement with Russia (30 days per 60-day period). EU continues to pressure BiH to align its visa policy but BiH has not yet acted. Multiple June 2026 sources (Balkan Insight, RFERL, EU Commission reports) confirm the regime persists. No change to the Russia entry.

2026-06-29 NOTE: Wikipedia's 'Visa policy of Bosnia and Herzegovina' page (last updated June 21 2026) still lists Kuwait as visa-free (90 days within 6 months). This is an error on Wikipedia — the Council of Ministers officially reintroduced visa requirements for Kuwait effective October 1 2025. The current BIH.json record (Kuwait removed from visa_free) is correct and consistent with the official BiH government decision.
gaps: The primary official MFA visa page (mvp.gov.ba/en/vize) and the Decision on Visas PDF were inaccessible due to SSL certificate errors; the nationality list was compiled from official BiH embassy websites (Norway, USA, Belgium) and Wikipedia's policy article.

Kuwait's removal (visa required from October 1, 2025) confirmed by multiple secondary sources referencing the Council of Ministers decision; not independently verified from mvp.gov.ba (SSL blocked). Note: the Norway embassy page (bhembassy.no) and Wikipedia (as of June 21 2026) still incorrectly list Kuwait as visa-free; both sources are outdated on this point. The official Council of Ministers decision is authoritative.

Bahrain/Oman/Saudi Arabia seasonal waiver for 2026: The 2026 renewal is now confirmed via the BiH MFA official Twitter/X account (@mfa_bih, April 2026, post ID 1914670295970963821) and corroborated by Wego (updated April 20, 2026). The source_url in conditional_access entries still references the 2025 mvp.gov.ba announcement as baseline (the 2026-specific mvp.gov.ba announcement URL was not directly accessible due to SSL errors). The MFA Twitter confirmation is treated as authoritative given it is the official MFA account.

Singapore visa-free status resolved: Both the Norway embassy live fetch (June 2026) and Wikipedia's BiH visa policy article list Singapore in the ordinary-passport visa-free category (90 days). The Brussels embassy page discrepancy appears to be a local drafting error. Singapore is correctly classified as visa_free.
No e-visa system exists for Bosnia and Herzegovina as of June 2026.
No ETA system exists for Bosnia and Herzegovina.
No digital nomad visa, startup visa, or fast-track talent scheme found on any official BiH government source.
No CBI program exists; the Article 15 'exceptional naturalization' provision is discretionary and has no official program page.

Minimum investment amounts for business-based residence are not officially published; the 1,000 BAM figure is the minimum share capital for company registration, not an immigration-specific investment requirement.
Dual citizenship policy for naturalized citizens not definitively confirmed from official sources reviewed.
Bosnia and Herzegovina has EU candidate status; not yet a member of EU/EEA or Schengen Area.
UK-issued visas and residence permits explicitly DO NOT qualify for the Schengen/EU/US credential rule (post-Brexit).
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or similar special-status cardholder rule found on any official BiH government source.
Exact max_stay_days for diplomatic/service passport exemptions not stated on official sources reviewed.
Belarus diplomatic/service passport exemption reported by some aggregators but not confirmed on official BiH embassy pages reviewed - not recorded.

Qatar's visa-free status (year-round 90 days) confirmed via secondary sources; it remains part of BiH's permanent bilateral agreements and was not affected by the EU harmonization measure that removed Kuwait.

Hong Kong SAR, Macao SAR, and Taiwan (ROC) travel document holders - Wikipedia lists some as visa-free but this could not be confirmed from official BiH embassy pages reviewed; not recorded as separate entries.

visa_types.name: Aerodromska Tranzitna Viza (Visa A) - Airport Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through the international transit zone of an airport in Bosnia and Herzegovina without entering the country proper. Issued for single, dual, or multiple airport transits.
visa_types.notes: Only required for nationals of certain countries who must transit through the airport international zone. The visa does not permit entry into BiH territory. Fee of 100 BAM applies if exceptionally issued at the border/airport. Applications made in advance at BiH embassies/consulates.

visa_types.name: Viza za Kratkoročni Boravak (Visa C) - Short-Stay Visa (Tourist/Business/Private)
visa_types.purpose: Covers short-term stays for tourism, business, private visits, education, cultural or sports events, and transit through Bosnia and Herzegovina territory. Allows up to 90 days within any 6-month period.
visa_types.notes: This is Bosnia and Herzegovina's primary short-stay visa category, equivalent to a Schengen C visa. Issued as single or multiple entry. Validity up to 1 year (extendable in exceptional circumstances). If issued exceptionally at a land border crossing or airport, a 100 BAM fee applies. Applications must be submitted at a BiH embassy or consulate at least one month before travel. Applicants must show minimum BAM 150 per day financial means. Foreign nationals must register their residence address within 48 hours of entry. Bosnia and Herzegovina does not operate an e-visa system - all applications are in-person.

visa_types.name: Viza za Kratkoročni Boravak (Visa C) - Short-Stay Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay business variant of Visa C covering business meetings, negotiations, conferences, and other commercial activities in Bosnia and Herzegovina for up to 90 days within a 6-month period.
visa_types.notes: Sub-purpose of Visa C. Requires an employer/sponsor letter and an invitation letter from a BiH-registered company verified by the BiH Ministry of Security. Same visa document as tourist Visa C; the purpose is distinguished by supporting documents. Bosnia and Herzegovina does not issue a separate business visa document.

visa_types.name: Viza za Kratkoročni Boravak (Visa C) - Short-Stay Family/Private Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay variant of Visa C for visiting family members or friends residing in Bosnia and Herzegovina, valid for up to 90 days within a 6-month period.
visa_types.notes: Sub-purpose of Visa C. Requires an invitation letter from the resident host in BiH, verified by relevant authorities, including a sponsor declaration of financial responsibility. The same Visa C document is issued; purpose is determined by supporting documents.

visa_types.name: Viza za Dugoročni Boravak (Visa D) - Long-Stay/National Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals intending to stay in Bosnia and Herzegovina for more than 90 days within a 6-month period, typically as a precursor to obtaining a temporary residence permit.
visa_types.notes: Bosnia and Herzegovina's long-stay national visa (Visa D) is required by those seeking temporary residence and must be obtained at a BiH embassy or consulate abroad before arriving. Issued for stays up to 6 months, validity up to 1 year. After entry on a D visa, applicants apply for a formal temporary residence permit (privremeni boravak) at the Service for Foreigners Affairs. The D visa covers purposes including employment, family reunification, education, humanitarian reasons, and real estate ownership. Bosnia and Herzegovina does not operate an e-visa system.

visa_types.name: Privremeni Boravak - Temporary Residence (Employment/Work)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to legally reside and work in Bosnia and Herzegovina for employment purposes, requiring both a temporary residence permit and a work permit in most cases.
visa_types.notes: First-time applicants must enter on a Visa D. Temporary residence for employment is issued for up to 1 year and is renewable. Requires a valid passport (3+ months validity beyond the requested period), proof of employment or work permit, accommodation proof, health insurance, and a medical certificate (within 3 months). Extensions must be filed at least 15 days before expiration. After 5 consecutive years of temporary residence, applicants may apply for permanent residence. Administrative fees apply (amounts referenced in official fee schedule). Company founders/owners may qualify for work without a formal work permit under a separate business owner category.

visa_types.name: Privremeni Boravak - Temporary Residence (Education/Study)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to reside in Bosnia and Herzegovina for the duration of an accredited educational programme at a BiH institution.
visa_types.notes: Issued as a category of Temporary Residence (privremeni boravak) on educational grounds. Entry via Visa D. Issued up to 1 year, renewable for the duration of study. Requires proof of enrolment, accommodation, financial means, and health insurance. After 5 years of uninterrupted temporary residence, permanent residence may be sought. Bosnia and Herzegovina has no separate dedicated student visa document - it is a purpose category within temporary residence.

visa_types.name: Privremeni Boravak - Temporary Residence (Family Reunification)
visa_types.purpose: Enables foreign nationals to reside in Bosnia and Herzegovina to join a family member who holds legal residence status in the country.
visa_types.notes: Category of Temporary Residence for family reunification. Entry via Visa D. Requires proof of family relationship, sponsor's valid residence in BiH, accommodation, and financial means. Issued up to 1 year, renewable. Path to permanent residence after 5 years continuous temporary residence.

visa_types.name: Stalni Boravak - Permanent Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Grants foreign nationals an indefinite right to reside in Bosnia and Herzegovina after meeting the required continuous residence period and integration conditions.
visa_types.notes: Requires minimum 5 years of continuous legal temporary residence in BiH immediately preceding the application. Additional requirements: valid passport, stable and regular income, adequate housing, health insurance, proficiency in at least one official BiH language (Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian), and criminal clearance from home country and BiH (certificates no older than 6 months). Upon approval, a unique identification number is assigned and the holder must apply for a resident ID card within 8 days. Administrative fee: 200 BAM referenced in SPS documentation. Permanent residence is not citizenship.

visa_types.name: Privremeni Boravak na osnovu vlasništva nekretnina - Temporary Residence Based on Real Estate Ownership
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals who own real estate in Bosnia and Herzegovina to obtain temporary residence, provided they demonstrate a real and lasting connection to the country.
visa_types.notes: No minimum property value is specified in law. Applicant must demonstrate a 'real and lasting connection' to BiH (e.g., origin, children's education in BiH, pension receipt, or investment in economic activities). Issued for up to 1 year, renewable. Path to permanent residence after 5 consecutive years of continuous temporary residence. Administrative fee: 150 BAM for issuance.

visa_types.name: Privremeni Boravak - Temporary Residence Based on Business/Company Establishment
visa_types.purpose: Permits founders or owners of a company registered in Bosnia and Herzegovina to reside in the country and conduct business activities without a separate work permit.
visa_types.notes: Classified as 'work without a work permit' for company founders/owners. No minimum investment threshold set for this residence category specifically; minimum registered share capital for a d.o.o. (LLC) in FBiH entity is 1,000 BAM (approx. EUR 511). Issued up to 1 year, renewable. Path to permanent residence after 5 consecutive years. Entry typically via Visa D.

visa_types.name: Diplomatska / Službena Viza - Diplomatic and Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of diplomatic or official/service passports from countries that do not have a full visa-free agreement with Bosnia and Herzegovina, permitting official travel and residence.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic and official/service passport holders from select countries (including Algeria, Armenia, China, Cuba, Egypt, Indonesia, Iran, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Morocco, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Tunisia, Ukraine, and Russia) are exempt from visa requirements. Diplomatic visa fee is generally waived based on reciprocity. Duration determined by mission length or bilateral agreement terms.

outbound_official.notes: Official Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Bosnia and Herzegovina visa page (mvp.gov.ba/bs/vize) lists per-country visa requirements for BiH citizens. "Obicna biometrijska putna isprava NE" = ordinary biometric passport, no visa required. Since BiH has issued only biometric passports since 2014, all 65 visa-free entries apply to current BiH ordinary passport holders. Russia: visa-free by bilateral agreement up to 30 days per 60-day period (confirmed in page text). Belarus: NE but limited to 5-day transit via Minsk airport. Tajikistan and Uzbekistan: NE for tourist stays up to 30 days. UAE: NE confirmed by bilateral MoU (90 days per 180-day period). Cambodia: visa obtainable on arrival or via e-visa. Indonesia: visa on arrival for tourism (30 days, ~USD 35). Nepal: visa on arrival. Cuba: tourist card required (not traditional visa).
